name: Ramanuja
dates_notes: Traditional Sri Vaishnava sectarian dates 1017–1137 give a 120-year lifespan that modern academic scholarship largely rejects. Carman (1974) and Lester argue for c. 1077–1157 (an 80-year lifespan); others place him a generation later still. The corpus retains the traditional range as the school-internal datum but flags the academic alternative as live.

lineage_polemical.note: Rāmānuja famously broke from his bhedābhedin teacher Yādava-Prakāśa; Viśiṣṭādvaita defines itself partly against this earlier position.
Rāmānuja's Śrī-Bhāṣya is the principal Viśiṣṭādvaitin polemic against Advaitic māyā via the sapta-vidhā-anupapatti.

Rāmānuja's Śrī-Bhāṣya on the utpatty-asambhavādhikaraṇa (BS 2.2.42–45) continues Yāmuna's defense: the vyūha-production is read as an internal differentiation of one Brahman-substance into manifestations rather than as the production of one substance from another. Pāñcarātra ritual remains the canonical liturgical frame of Śrīvaiṣṇava worship throughout.

core_thesis: Rāmānuja gives Viśiṣṭādvaita its school-defining form (Clooney's *Theology After Vedānta*, 1993, is the standard English-language secondary reading of the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* methodology). Brahman — Nārāyaṇa-with-Śrī — is the sole independent reality, but its unity is *viśiṣṭa* (qualified), not undifferentiated. The infinite plurality of conscious selves (*cit*) and non-conscious matter (*acit*) constitutes Brahman's body (*śarīra*); Brahman is their *śarīrin* and *antaryāmin* (inner controller). *Cit* and *acit* are really distinct from each other and from Brahman, yet inseparable (*apṛthak-siddha*) — they cannot exist or be conceived apart from him. There is therefore no unqualified *nirviśeṣa* Brahman; *neti neti* negates limiting attributes, not all distinction. Against Śaṅkara's *māyāvāda* the [*Vedārtha-Saṃgraha* §76](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/76) ff. unfolds the *sapta-vidhānupapatti* — seven distinct destructive arguments against Advaitin *avidyā*. Two examples make the structure visible. (i) *Āśrayānupapatti*: locate avidyā's substrate. If in Brahman, Brahman ceases to be self-luminous *cit*; if in the *jīva*, the *jīva* is itself a product of *avidyā* and the explanation circles. (ii) *Tirodhānānupapatti*: avidyā is supposed to conceal Brahman; but Brahman is by definition *svayam-prakāśa*, so concealment of Brahman is concealment of light by darkness as such — an event with no possible mechanism. The remaining five (against avidyā's nature, indeterminability, *pramāṇa*, *nivartaka*, and cessation) operate by the same destructive technique: take the Advaitin's own characterization, run it through its consequences, exhibit the contradiction. The constructive position then follows. The *jīva* is an atomic, eternal *jñātṛ* (knower) with *dharmi-bhūta-jñāna* — substance-awareness bearing real attributes ([*Vedārtha-Saṃgraha* §§88–93](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/88-93); *Śrī-Bhāṣya* on [*Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.18](cite://badarayana/brahma-sutra/2.3.18) ff.). *Tat tvam asi* is read on the body-soul model of *sāmānādhikaraṇya* ([*Vedārtha-Saṃgraha* §§19–34](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/19-34), glossing *Chāndogya* 6.8.7): *tat* and *tvam* co-predicate one substratum because Brahman is the inner self of the *jīva*, not because they are numerically identical. *Mokṣa* is the *jīva*'s perfected *kaiṅkarya* (beatific service) to Nārāyaṇa, not the dissolution of personhood (*Śrī-Bhāṣya* on [*Brahma-Sūtra* 4.4](cite://badarayana/brahma-sutra/4.4), the *mukti-pāda*). *Bhakti-yoga* — meditative devotion as enjoined *upāsanā* — is the constitutive form of *mokṣa* (*Śrī-Bhāṣya* on [*Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.1](cite://badarayana/brahma-sutra/1.1.1), the *Mahā-Siddhānta*'s closing *bhakti-rūpāpannā smṛti-santati*); *prapatti* (utter self-surrender) is a parallel path, open especially to those incapable of sustained *bhakti-yoga* (*Gītā-Bhāṣya* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 18.66, the *carama-śloka*; *Śaraṇāgati-Gadya*).

engaged_works.title: Shri-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the school-defining commentary on the Brahma-Sūtra.
engaged_works.summary: The school-defining commentary on Bādarāyaṇa's *Brahma-Sūtra*, written (per tradition) on the basis of Bodhāyana's *Vṛtti* and the Drāviḍa lineage. The Sanskrit text is now on disk in clean form (`materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/_replaced/_pure_sanskrit/ramanuja_sribhashya__pure-skt.txt`); Clooney's *Theology After Vedānta* (1993) remains the standard English-language secondary reading of the work as a constructive theological argument. The *Mahā-Siddhānta* on *sūtra* 1.1.1 (*athāto brahmajijñāsā*) opens with the *Mahā-Pūrvapakṣa* — an unusually full statement of the Advaitic position, conceded its strongest form before refutation — followed by the *sapta-vidhānupapatti* against *avidyā*. The seven destructive arguments target, in turn: the locus (*āśraya*) of avidyā, its concealment (*tirodhāna*) of self-luminous Brahman, its essential nature (*svarūpa*), its alleged indeterminability (*anirvacanīyatva*), the *pramāṇa* by which it would be cognised, the *nivartaka* by which it would be removed, and the form its cessation (*nivṛtti*) would take. Each argument operates by taking the Advaitin's characterization as a premise and exhibiting the contradiction it generates; the cumulative effect is to deny avidyā any coherent place in the system that posits it. The *Śrī-Bhāṣya* then builds the positive Viśiṣṭādvaita reading *sūtra* by *sūtra*. The first *adhyāya*'s *samanvaya* establishes that every *śruti*-passage, *tat tvam asi* included, predicates Brahman as qualified by the world of *cit* and *acit* — the *sāmānādhikaraṇya* (co-predication) is the body-soul *sāmānādhikaraṇya*, not numerical identity. The work matters because it converts Yāmuna's *Siddhi*-positions into a complete *sūtra*-anchored alternative to Śaṅkara within the same shared exegetical genre.

engaged_works.title: Vedartha-Samgraha
engaged_works.summary: An independent *prakaraṇa* giving the Viśiṣṭādvaita reading of the *Upaniṣad*-corpus directly, without the *sūtra*-frame. Its central target is the Advaitic exegesis of *tat tvam asi*. Rāmānuja's argument: *tat* and *tvam* are not unqualified denoters; *tat* refers to Brahman-as-qualified-by-the-world and *tvam* to the *jīva*-as-embodied-self, both with determinate references. Two terms with determinate references can stand in *sāmānādhikaraṇya* — co-predication of one substratum — only on the body-soul model, where one term names the inner self of what the other names. Hence the *mahā-vākya* teaches non-difference *as* inseparability (*apṛthak-siddhi*) of self and inner-self, not numerical identity; reading it as identity equivocates on *sāmānādhikaraṇya*. The [*Vedārtha-Saṃgraha* §76](cite://ramanuja/vedartha-samgraha/76) ff. then develops the *sapta-vidhānupapatti* against *avidyā* — seven destructive arguments engaging avidyā's *āśraya*, its *tirodhāna* of self-luminous Brahman, its *svarūpa*, its *anirvacanīyatva*, its *pramāṇa*, its *nivartaka*, and its *nivṛtti*. The same treatise constructs *dharmi-bhūta-jñāna* (substance-awareness bearing real attributes) as the analytic of consciousness, and engages two intermediate Vedāntic positions explicitly: Yādava-Prakāśa's *bhedābheda* and Bhāskara's *aupādhika* identity-difference. The work is the most accessible single statement of the school's positive metaphysics and its constructive scriptural hermeneutics.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Dipa
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored compact commentary on the Brahma-Sūtra.
engaged_works.summary: A medium-length commentary on the *Brahma-Sūtra*, giving a compact exposition of the same Viśiṣṭādvaita reading developed at length in the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*. The structural moves are preserved — *samanvaya* of *śruti* on Brahman *saviśeṣa*, the *śarīra-śarīrin* relation as the key to *sāmānādhikaraṇya*, the rejection of *nirviśeṣa* Brahman and *avidyā* — but the polemical *pūrvapakṣa* and the long engagements with Bhāskara and Yādava-Prakāśa are trimmed. The *Vedānta-Dīpa* serves as the bridge between the encyclopaedic *Śrī-Bhāṣya* and the still shorter *Vedānta-Sāra*: it is what advanced students used once the framework was internalized. Rāmānuja's willingness to recapitulate the entire *sūtra*-pāṭha three times at three lengths — *Bhāṣya*, *Dīpa*, *Sāra* — testifies to his pedagogical seriousness about transmission. The school *teaches* through the *Dīpa* what it *establishes* through the *Bhāṣya*.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Sara
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored shorter commentary on the Brahma-Sūtra.
engaged_works.summary: The shortest of Rāmānuja's three *Brahma-Sūtra* commentaries — a pithy *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* gloss giving the bare Viśiṣṭādvaita reading without extended *pūrvapakṣa* or polemic. Designed for memorisation and oral transmission, the *Vedānta-Sāra* is the entry-text through which a student internalizes the school's *adhikaraṇa*-divisions and the determinate gloss of each *sūtra* before moving to the *Dīpa* and the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*. The brevity itself carries a doctrinal claim: once the body-soul reading of *sāmānādhikaraṇya* is grasped, the Viśiṣṭādvaita reading of the *sūtra*-s is the obvious one and the basic gloss can be transmitted in compressed form. The work is the canonical pedagogical entry-point to Rāmānuja's *sūtra*-exegesis and the clearest evidence of his structuring of the school's curriculum at three escalating levels of detail.

engaged_works.title: Gita-Bhashya
engaged_works.summary: Commentary on the *Bhagavad-Gītā* organizing the eighteen chapters into three *ṣaṭka*-s on *karma*, *jñāna*, and *bhakti*, with *bhakti-yoga* as the culminating *yoga*. The decisive contention is against the Advaitic reading that takes the *Gītā* to teach *jñāna* to which devotion is propaedeutic. Rāmānuja's reading is the inverse: *bhakti-yoga* is itself the operative form of mature *upāsanā* — meditative devotion to Kṛṣṇa-as-Para-Brahman that, once intensified to the perspicuity of direct presence (*sākṣātkāra*), *is* the liberating cognition. The hinge is the gloss on the *carama-śloka* (*Gītā* 18.66, *sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja*), which Rāmānuja reads as enjoining *prapatti* — utter self-surrender — as a parallel and equally efficacious path for those incapable of sustained *bhakti-yoga*. The two-path soteriology of the school is grounded here, in the *Gītā* itself, and not as a later innovation. The commentary also supplies the definitive Vaiṣṇava counter-reading of every passage Śaṅkara had taken to teach world-renouncing monism.

engaged_works.title: Gadya-Trayam
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Traditionally ascribed and accepted in tradition; modern scholarship occasionally questions individual passages.
engaged_works.summary: Three short Sanskrit prose-hymns — *Śaraṇāgati-Gadya*, *Śrīraṅga-Gadya*, *Vaikuṇṭha-Gadya* — traditionally composed at Śrīraṅgam on the Pañcamī of the bright fortnight of Phālguna, in which Rāmānuja enacts his own *prapatti* to Śrī-Raṅganātha and Śrī. The *Śaraṇāgati-Gadya* is the locus classicus for the school's articulation of *prapatti* as self-surrender qualified by the six *aṅga*-s: *ānukūlya-saṅkalpa* (intent toward the favorable), *prātikūlya-varjana* (avoidance of the contrary), *rakṣiṣyatīti viśvāsa* (trust that he will protect), *goptṛtva-varaṇa* (election of him as protector), *ātma-nikṣepa* (casting down of the self), *kārpaṇya* (acknowledged helplessness) — and performed in the presence of Śrī as *puruṣakāra*. The *Śrīraṅga-Gadya* and *Vaikuṇṭha-Gadya* extend the act into ongoing *kaiṅkarya* and contemplation of the Lord's *parama-pada*. The texts bridge Rāmānuja the systematician and the school's lived devotional practice. They later become contested ground in the Vaḍakalai-Tenkalai split, where the same six *aṅga*-s are read in two incompatible ways: as conditions the surrendered self must enact (Vaḍakalai, Vedānta Deśika) or as descriptions of what surrender already is (Tenkalai, Pillai-Lokācārya). The text itself is shared; the construal is not.

engaged_works.title: Nitya-Grantha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Traditionally ascribed manual of daily worship.
engaged_works.summary: A short Sanskrit manual prescribing the daily *ārādhana* of Nārāyaṇa for the qualified Śrīvaiṣṇava: morning *snāna*, *sandhyā*, *pūjā*, the *pañca-kāla* five-fold division of the day into Vaiṣṇava observances, and the *mantra*-recitation appropriate to each. The *Nitya-Grantha* operationalises the contemplative life implied by *bhakti-yoga*. Meditative remembrance of Bhagavān is to be sustained not as an episodic spiritual exercise but as the form of the day itself. The text codifies the Pāñcarātra-derived *mantra*-system used in Śrīvaiṣṇava temple and household worship and integrates it with smārta *nitya-karma*. It matters because Viśiṣṭādvaita, on Rāmānuja's account, is not merely a philosophical position but a *vidhi* — an enjoined form of life — and the *Nitya-Grantha* is what makes the position liveable as daily structure. It is also the textual basis for the liturgy still in use in the Śrīvaiṣṇava temple system. The traditional ascription is secure; modern scholarship raises minor source-critical questions on individual passages but accepts core authorship.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same substrate structure axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `one-self-standing` rather than `one-qualified-by-real-internal-distinctions`.
Adi Sankara presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `sublatable-not-null` rather than `dependent-real`.
Anandabodha presses the same identity relation axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `non-otherness` rather than `body-soul-qualification`.
Akalanka presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `plural-pramana-realism` rather than `scripture-dominant`.
Anantakrishna Sastri presses the same truth-structure axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `hierarchical-standpoint-truth` rather than `single-absolute-truth`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same normativity axis in the ethical register, but with the value `ethical-life-in-institutions` rather than `divine-command-or-grace`.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `knowledge-discipline` rather than `devotion-and-grace`.
Alfred North Whitehead presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `not-soteriological` rather than `service-with-distinction-preserved`.
Baladeva Vidyabhushana presses the same causation model axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `unchanged-ground-with-changing-power` rather than `body-soul-causation`.
Bannanje Govindacharya presses the same individuation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `irreducible-dependent` rather than `qualified-mode`.
